html:
[404]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 兵守土门 - Refers to the military defense of a strategic gate or pass.
  • 真卿兄杲卿为常山太守 - Indicates that Zhang Xuansu's brother, Zhang Gaqing, served as the governor of Changshan.
  • 共破土门十七郡同日归顺 - Means 'together they defeated the defenders of the seventeen prefectures and surrendered on the same day.'
  • 推真卿为帅 - Means 'to appoint Zhang Xuansu as the leader.'
  • 得兵二十万 - Means 'gained an army of 200,000 troops.'
  • 横絶燕赵 - Means 'to cut across the territories of Yan and Zhao.'
  • 诏加戸部侍郎平原太守 - Means 'the emperor decreed that he be promoted to the position of Minister of Revenue and Governor of Pingyuan.'
  • 清河郡客李蕚谒于军前 - Means 'a guest from Qinghe Prefecture, Li E, paid homage before the army.'
  • 共破禄山党二万余人于堂邑 - Means 'together they defeated more than 20,000 followers of An Lushan at Tangyi.'
  • 肃宗幸灵武 - Means 'Emperor Suzong visited Lingwu.'
  • 诏授工部尚书御史大夫 - Means 'the emperor decreed that he be appointed Minister of Works and Grand Censor.'
  • 真卿间道朝于鳯翔 - Means 'Zhang Xuansu took a secret route to pay his respects in Fengxiang.'
  • 拜宪部尚书 - Means 'was appointed Minister of Justice.'
  • 寻加御史大夫 - Means 'soon after, he was promoted to Grand Censor.'
  • 弹奏黜陟朝纲大举 - Means 'he initiated investigations and appointments, greatly reforming the court's administration.'
  • 连典蒲州同州 - Means 'he consecutively governed Pu Prefecture and Tong Prefecture.'
  • 皆有遗爱 - Means 'all had a lasting legacy of good will.'
  • 为御史唐实所搆 - Means 'was framed by the censor Tang Shi.'
  • 宰臣所忌 - Means 'feared by the prime minister.'
  • 贬饶州刺史 - Means 'was demoted to the position of Prefect of Rao Prefecture.'
  • 复拜升州浙西节度使 - Means 'was then appointed Governor-General of Shang Prefecture and Commander of the Western Zhejiang Circuit.'
  • 征为刑部尚书 - Means 'was summoned to serve as Minister of Justice.'
  • 又为李辅国所譛 - Means 'was slandered by Li Fuguo.'
  • 贬蓬州长史 - Means 'was demoted to the position of Chief Clerk of Peng Prefecture.'
  • 代宗嗣位 - Means 'Emperor Daizong ascended the throne.'
  • 拜利州刺史 - Means 'was appointed Prefect of L﻿i Prefecture.'

[405]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 皆先白长官 - Formal expression meaning 'all must first report to the local officials.'
  • 长官白宰相 - Means 'the local officials then report to the prime minister.'
  • 然后上闻 - Means 'and only then does the emperor hear about it.'
  • 真卿奏疏极言之 - Means 'Duan Zhenqing submitted a memorial strongly arguing against this.'
  • 乃止 - Means 'and thus it was stopped.'
  • 后因摄祭太庙 - Means 'Later, when he was temporarily in charge of the ancestral temple sacrifices.'
  • 以祭器不修言于朝 - Means 'he criticized the state of the sacrificial vessels in court.'
  • 元载以为诽谤时政 - Means 'Yuan Zai considered this a slander against the government.'
  • 贬硖州别驾 - Means 'and Duan Zhenqing was demoted to the position of Deputy Governor of Xia State.'
  • 复为抚州湖州刺史 - Means 'and later was appointed Governor of Fuzhou and Huzhou.'
  • 元载伏诛 - Means 'Yuan Zai was executed.'
  • 拜刑部尚书 - Means 'and Duan Zhenqing was appointed Minister of Justice.'
  • 代宗崩 - Means 'when Emperor Dezong died.'
  • 为礼仪使 - Means 'he was appointed Master of Ceremonies.'
  • 上议请取初諡为定 - Means 'he proposed in a memorial to retain only the original posthumous titles.'
  • 为宰相杨炎所忌 - Means 'but this was resented by the prime minister Yang Yan.'
  • 不行 - Means 'and the proposal was not implemented.'
  • 改太子少傅 - Means 'and Duan Zhenqing was instead appointed Vice Tutor of the Crown Prince.'
  • 潜夺其权 - Means 'but his power was subtly stripped away.'
  • 又改太子太师 - Means 'and he was again appointed Tutor of the Crown Prince.'
  • 时李希烈陷汝州 - Means 'when Li Xilie captured the state of Ruzhou.'
  • 宰相卢杞素忌其刚正 - Means 'the prime minister Lu Qi had long resented Duan Zhenqing's integrity.'
  • 将中害之 - Means 'and intended to harm him.'
  • 使往谕希烈可不血刃而平大寇矣 - Means 'and sent him to persuade Li Xilie, who could thus be pacified without bloodshed.'
  • 上从之 - Means 'the emperor agreed.'
  • 事行朝野失色 - Means 'when the mission was announced, the court and the country were shocked.'
  • 李勉闻之以为失一国老贻朝廷羞 - Means 'Li Mian, hearing of this, believed it was a loss to the state and a shame to the court.'
  • 密表请留 - Means 'and secretly submitted a memorial requesting that Duan Zhenqing be retained.'
  • 又遣人逆之于路 - Means 'and sent someone to intercept him on the way.'
  • 不及 - Means 'but it was too late.'
  • 既见希烈方宣诏防 - Means 'and when Duan Zhenqing arrived, Li Xilie was in the process of announcing his own edict.'
  • 希烈养子千余人雪刃争前欲杀之 - Means 'Li Xilie's adopted sons, armed with swords, rushed forward to kill him.'
  • 丛绕 - Means 'and surrounded him.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元载 - Yuan Zai, a powerful chancellor during the Tang Dynasty known for his corruption.
  • 杨炎 - Yang Yan, a chancellor during the Tang Dynasty who opposed Duan Zhenqing's proposal.
  • 卢杞 - Lu Qi, a chancellor during the Tang Dynasty who was known for his political intrigue and cruelty.
  • 李希烈 - Li Xilie, a rebel leader during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 汝州 - A state in the Tang Dynasty, located in present-day Henan Province.
  • 刑部尚书 - The head of the Ministry of Justice in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 礼仪使 - The Master of Ceremonies, responsible for imperial rituals and ceremonies.
  • 太子少傅 - Vice Tutor of the Crown Prince, an educational and advisory role.
  • 太子太师 - Tutor of the Crown Prince, a higher educational and advisory role.

Proper Nouns

  • 元载 - Yuan Zai, a powerful chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 杨炎 - Yang Yan, a chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 卢杞 - Lu Qi, a chancellor during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 李希烈 - Li Xilie, a rebel leader during the late Tang Dynasty.
  • 汝州 - A state in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 刑部尚书 - The head of the Ministry of Justice in the Tang Dynasty.
  • 礼仪使 - The Master of Ceremonies.
  • 太子少傅 - Vice Tutor of the Crown Prince.
  • 太子太师 - Tutor of the Crown Prince.

[406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 诟骂神色不动 - Phrase describing someone who remains calm and composed even when being insulted or criticized.
  • 希烈以身蔽之 - 'Xiliang shielded him with his own body,' indicating a protective action.
  • 乃就馆舍 - 'He then went to the lodging,' suggesting a change in location or activity.
  • 希烈因宴其党 - 'Xiliang then feasted his followers,' indicating a gathering or celebration.
  • 使倡优讟朝政 - 'He had the entertainers criticize the government,' where '讟' means to slander or criticize.
  • 相公人臣也 - 'Xianggong, you are a subject of the emperor,' addressing someone respectfully but reminding them of their position.
  • 奈何使小辈如此 - 'Why are you making the lower people behave like this?' expressing disapproval of Xiliang's actions.
  • 遂起 - 'He then stood up,' indicating a reaction to the situation.
  • 其逆党救之 - 'His rebels saved him,' referring to those loyal to Xiliang.
  • 真卿乃自作遗表墓志祭文 - 'Zhenqing then wrote his own will, epitaph, and eulogy,' preparing for his death.
  • 吾尝修道以形全为先 - 'I have always practiced Daoism, prioritizing the preservation of my body,' indicating a belief in Daoist philosophy.
  • 吾死之后但割吾 - 'After my death, just cut off my...' (the sentence is incomplete, but it likely refers to a specific part of his body).
  • 贼党使缢之 - 'The rebels had him strangled,' indicating the method of execution.
  • 兴元元年八月三日也 - 'On the third day of August in the first year of Xingyuan,' providing the date of Zhenqing's death.
  • 朝廷闻之辍朝五日 - 'The court heard of it and stopped mourning for five days,' indicating the official response to his death.
  • 谥文忠公 - 'He was posthumously given the title 'Wenzhong Gong,' indicating his official honorific title.
  • 为卢杞所排 - 'He was sidelined by Lu Qi,' indicating political persecution.
  • 别传云 - 'According to another account,' indicating a secondary source or additional information.
  • 真卿将缢解金带以遗使者 - 'Zhenqing, about to be strangled, removed his gold belt to give to the messenger,' indicating a final act before his death.

[407]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 支節血為吾吭血以绐之 - Complex idiom. '支節血' refers to blood from a limb or part of the body, '為吾吭血' means 'as my throat blood' (i.e., my lifeblood or my own blood), and '以绐之' means 'to deceive him with it.' The whole phrase may be a metaphorical or ritualistic expression of sacrifice or deception through self-sacrifice.
  • 吾死无所恨矣 - Standard classical expression meaning 'I will have no regrets upon my death.'
  • 缢者如其言 - 'The hanged one did as he said,' referring to someone who was executed by hanging and carried out the instructions given.
  • 棺朽败而尸形俨然 - 'The coffin was decayed and broken, yet the body's form was still solemn and intact.' This is a striking image of preservation.
  • 肌肉如生 - 'The muscles were as if still alive,' emphasizing the unnatural preservation of the body.
  • 手足柔软 - 'Hands and feet were soft,' suggesting the body had not yet stiffened.
  • 髭髪青黑 - 'Beard and hair were black and dark,' indicating youth and vitality.
  • 握拳不开 - 'Fist clenched and would not open,' a detail that adds to the mystery of the preserved body.
  • 爪透手背 - 'Claws pierced the back of the hand,' possibly metaphorical or literal, but unusual in classical texts.
  • 远近惊异焉 - 'People far and near were astonished,' indicating the widespread reaction to the phenomenon.
  • 行及中路旅榇渐轻 - 'As the journey reached the middle road, the funeral casket grew lighter,' suggesting supernatural or miraculous change.
  • 后达葬所空棺而已 - 'Later, upon arriving at the burial place, it was only an empty coffin,' the final confirmation of the miraculous event.
  • 开天传信记详而载焉 - 'The *Kaitian Zhuanxin Ji* (Book of Heavenly Transmission and Faithful Records) recorded this in detail.'
  • 别传又云 - 'Another account says' or 'It is also said in a separate biography.'
  • 吾与元载俱服上药 - 'I and Yuanzai both took the same elixirs,' referring to a comparison of their fates.
  • 彼为酒色所败 - 'He was ruined by wine and women,' a standard classical expression of moral decline.
  • 此去蔡州必为逆贼所害 - 'This journey to Cizhou will surely bring me to be harmed by the traitorous rebels.'
  • 尔后可迎吾丧于华隂 - 'Afterward, you may bring my funeral to Huayin.'
  • 开棺果覩其异 - 'Upon opening the coffin, they indeed saw the marvel.'
  • 形仙者也 - 'This is a 'form immortal' or 'a being of physical immortality.'
  • 虽藏于铁石之中 - 'Even if hidden within iron or stone,' suggesting the body's preservation is beyond normal conditions.
  • 链形数满自当擘裂飞去矣 - 'Once the number of transformations is complete, it will naturally split and fly away.' A reference to alchemical or Daoist transformation.
  • 顔氏之家自雍遣家仆往郑州征庄租廻及洛京 - Complex sentence. 'The family of Yan, from Yong, sent a household servant to Zhengzhou to collect the land rent, who returned to Luoyang.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 元载 - A high-ranking Tang official and chancellor known for corruption and eventually executed in 777 CE. His death is contrasted with Yan Zhenqing's.
  • 上药 - 'Upper medicine,' a term for elixirs or alchemical substances believed to prolong life or grant immortality.
  • 逆贼 - 'Rebellious traitors' or 'traitorous rebels,' a term used in classical Chinese to refer to those who opposed the emperor or committed treason.
  • 华隂 - A place name in Shaanxi province, historically associated with Daoist and spiritual traditions.
  • 邢和璞 - A Daoist scholar and alchemist of the Tang dynasty known for his knowledge of spiritual and physical transformation.
  • 形仙 - A term for a 'form immortal' or 'physical immortal,' referring to someone who has achieved immortality through physical means, not spiritual transcendence.
  • 链形 - 'Transformation of form,' a term from Daoist alchemy referring to the process of refining the body into a more immortal state.
  • 开天传信记 - A historical or hagiographical text that records miraculous or divine events. This is likely a fictional or semi-historical work.
  • 顔氏 - Refers to the family of Yan Zhenqing, a prominent Tang dynasty family known for loyalty and virtue.
  • 雍 - A place name, possibly referring to Yongzhou or a region in the west of China, from which the Yan family sent a servant.
  • 郑州 - A city in Henan province.
  • 洛京 - A term for Luoyang, the imperial capital of the Tang dynasty.

Proper Nouns

  • 元载 - A historical figure, Tang dynasty chancellor and corrupt official.
  • 邢和璞 - A Daoist scholar and alchemist of the Tang dynasty.
  • 华隂 - A place name in Shaanxi province.
  • 郑州 - A city in Henan province.
  • 洛京 - A term for Luoyang, the imperial capital of the Tang dynasty.
  • 雍 - A place name, possibly referring to a region in western China.
  • 开天传信记 - A historical or hagiographical text.
  • 蔡州 - A place name in Henan province.
  • 顔氏 - Refers to the family of Yan Zhenqing.

[408]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 偶到同徳寺 - Means 'chanced upon Tongde Temple,' indicating a casual visit.
  • 衣長白衫 - Describes wearing a long white robe, a common attire for monks or scholars.
  • 張蓋 - Refers to holding an umbrella or canopy, often associated with high-ranking officials or dignitaries.
  • 在佛殿上坐 - Means 'sitting on the Buddha hall,' suggesting a position of respect or authority.
  • 此仆遽欲近前拜之 - Means 'this servant hurriedly wanted to approach and bow to him,' indicating a sudden impulse to show respect.
  • 公遂转身去 - Means 'the master then turned away,' suggesting a deliberate action.
  • 仰观佛壁 - Means 'looking up at the Buddha wall,' describing a specific action.
  • 左右随之 - Means 'following him to the left and right,' indicating a trailing behavior.
  • 终不令仆见其面 - Means 'never allowing the servant to see his face,' emphasizing a deliberate avoidance.
  • 乃下佛殿出寺去 - Means 'then descended from the Buddha hall and left the temple,' indicating a departure.
  • 仆亦步随之 - Means 'the servant also followed step by step,' showing persistent tracking.
  • 径归城东北隅菜园中 - Means 'directly returned to the northeast corner of the city, to a vegetable garden,' specifying a location.
  • 有两间破屋 - Means 'there were two dilapidated houses,' describing the setting.
  • 门上悬箔子 - Means 'a curtain was hung on the door,' indicating a simple barrier.
  • 公便揭箔而入 - Means 'the master then lifted the curtain and entered,' describing an action.
  • 仆遂隔箔子唱喏 - Means 'the servant then bowed through the curtain,' indicating a formal greeting.
  • 公曰何人 - Means 'the master asked who it was,' showing a query.
  • 仆对以名 - Means 'the servant replied with his name,' indicating a response.
  • 公曰入来 - Means 'the master said, 'Come in,'' inviting the servant inside.
  • 仆既入拜輙拟哭 - Means 'after entering and bowing, the servant was about to cry,' indicating an emotional reaction.
  • 公遽止之 - Means 'the master quickly stopped him,' showing a sudden intervention.
  • 公探怀中出金十两 - Means 'the master reached into his chest and took out ten taels of gold,' describing an action.
  • 付仆以救家费 - Means 'gave it to the servant as a family expense,' indicating a practical purpose.
  • 仍遣速去 - Means 'then sent him away quickly,' showing a command.
  • 归勿与人説 - Means 'go back and do not tell anyone,' indicating a request for secrecy.
  • 后家内阙即再来 - Means 'if there is a need in the future, come again,' showing a conditional promise.
  • 仆还雍其家大惊 - Means 'when the servant returned to his home, his family was greatly surprised,' indicating a reaction.
  • 货其金乃真金也 - Means 'when they sold the gold, it turned out to be genuine gold,' confirming the value.
  • 顔氏子便市鞍马与向仆疾来省觐 - Means 'the son of Yan family then bought saddles and horses and came quickly to visit,' indicating a swift response.
  • 复至前处但满眼榛芜一无所有 - Means 'when they returned to the previous place, it was only full of thorns and weeds, with nothing there,' describing a changed scene.
  • 时人皆称鲁公尸解得道焉 - Means 'people at the time all called Master Lu's physical departure a spiritual attainment,' indicating a belief in his enlightenment.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 鲁公 - Refers to Master Lu, a figure of respect or authority.
  • 佛殿 - Refers to the Buddha hall, a place of worship.
  • 仆 - Refers to a servant or low-ranking individual.
  • 尸解得道 - A term in Taoist tradition referring to a form of spiritual transformation or enlightenment, where the physical body is left behind while the spirit ascends.

Proper Nouns

  • 鲁公 - Master Lu.
  • 佛殿 - Buddha hall.
  • 仆 - Servant.
  • 颜氏子 - Son of Yan family.

[409]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太平广记 - A large collection of stories and anecdotes from various sources, compiled during the Song Dynasty.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The official compilation of the Imperial Library during the Qing Dynasty, comprising a vast number of texts.
  • 神仙 - Refers to immortals or deities in Chinese mythology.
  • 游蜀 - Traveling to the region of Shu (今四川省一带).
  • 春暮 - Late spring.
  • 胜景 - Beautiful scenery.
  • 访异 - Seeking out unusual or extraordinary sights.
  • 游宴 - Enjoying feasts and banquets.
  • 郡南十里许 - About ten miles south of the county.
  • 郑氏林亭 - A garden pavilion belonging to the Zheng family.
  • 花卉方茂 - Flowers and plants are flourishing.
  • 出尘之胜 - A scene that transcends the mundane.
  • 端室巍巍 - A grand and imposing main hall.
  • 横然四峙 - Standing majestically on all sides.
  • 山门花辟 - The entrance gate is adorned with flowers.
  • 曲径烟矗 - A winding path surrounded by mist.
  • 延弇升巨亭之上 - Inviting 韦弇 to ascend the large pavilion.
  • 廻廊环构 - A circular corridor encircling the pavilion.
  • 饰以珠玉 - Adorned with pearls and jade.
  • 仙子十数 - Dozens of fairy maidens.
  • 左右侍衞 - Attendants on either side.
  • 华裾靓妆 - Elegant and beautiful attire.
  • 愿聊奉一醉 - Offering a brief drinking session.
  • 无以延欵为疑也 - Not doubting the hospitality.
  • 张乐饮酒 - Playing music and drinking.
  • 陈设肴膳 - Arranging food and dishes.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 太平广记 - A comprehensive collection of stories and anecdotes, often used as a reference for historical and mythological narratives.
  • 钦定四库全书 - A monumental work of the Qing Dynasty, reflecting the cultural and literary achievements of the time.
  • 神仙 - Immortals or deities in Chinese mythology, often associated with Daoist traditions.
  • 韦弇 - A historical figure, possibly a scholar or official, whose life and deeds are recorded in the text.
  • 申元之 - Another historical figure, possibly a contemporary or associate of 韦弇.
  • 马自然 - A third historical figure, possibly a contemporary or associate of 韦弇 and 申元之.
  • 张巨君 - A fourth historical figure, possibly a contemporary or associate of the others.
  • 游蜀 - Traveling to the region of Shu, which was known for its natural beauty and cultural significance.
  • 春暮 - Late spring, a time when nature is in full bloom.
  • 胜景 - Beautiful scenery, often associated with poetic and literary descriptions.
  • 访异 - Seeking out unusual or extraordinary sights, reflecting a desire for adventure and discovery.
  • 游宴 - Enjoying feasts and banquets, a common social activity in ancient China.
  • 郡南十里许 - About ten miles south of the county, indicating a specific location.
  • 郑氏林亭 - A garden pavilion belonging to the Zheng family, a place of beauty and tranquility.
  • 花卉方茂 - Flowers and plants are flourishing, describing the lush environment.
  • 出尘之胜 - A scene that transcends the mundane, suggesting a place of spiritual or supernatural significance.
  • 端室巍巍 - A grand and imposing main hall, indicating the importance of the pavilion.
  • 横然四峙 - Standing majestically on all sides, emphasizing the architectural grandeur.
  • 山门花辟 - The entrance gate is adorned with flowers, adding to the beauty of the entrance.
  • 曲径烟矗 - A winding path surrounded by mist, creating a mystical atmosphere.
  • 延弇升巨亭之上 - Inviting 韦弇 to ascend the large pavilion, a gesture of respect and hospitality.
  • 廻廊环构 - A circular corridor encircling the pavilion, enhancing the sense of space and elegance.
  • 饰以珠玉 - Adorned with pearls and jade, indicating the luxurious decoration.
  • 仙子十数 - Dozens of fairy maidens, representing the presence of supernatural beings.
  • 左右侍衞 - Attendants on either side, maintaining the order and decorum of the event.
  • 华裾靓妆 - Elegant and beautiful attire, reflecting the refined appearance of the fairies.
  • 中有一人与弇语 - One of them spoke to 韦弇, indicating a personal interaction.
  • 愿聊奉一醉 - Offering a brief drinking session, a gesture of friendship and hospitality.
  • 无以延欵为疑也 - Not doubting the hospitality, expressing trust and appreciation.
  • 张乐饮酒 - Playing music and drinking, a common form of entertainment in ancient times.
  • 陈设肴膳 - Arranging food and dishes, indicating the preparation for a feast.

Proper Nouns

  • 太平广记 - A large collection of stories and anecdotes.
  • 钦定四库全书 - The official compilation of the Imperial Library.
  • 神仙 - Immortals or deities.
  • 韦弇 - A historical figure.
  • 申元之 - Another historical figure.
  • 马自然 - A third historical figure.
  • 张巨君 - A fourth historical figure.
  • 郑氏林亭 - A garden pavilion belonging to the Zheng family.
  • 开元 - The reign title of Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 蜀 - The region of Shu (今四川省一带).
  • 春暮 - Late spring.
  • 胜景 - Beautiful scenery.
  • 访异 - Seeking out unusual or extraordinary sights.
  • 游宴 - Enjoying feasts and banquets.
  • 郡南十里许 - About ten miles south of the county.
  • 郑氏林亭 - A garden pavilion belonging to the Zheng family.
  • 花卉方茂 - Flowers and plants are flourishing.
  • 出尘之胜 - A scene that transcends the mundane.
  • 端室巍巍 - A grand and imposing main hall.
  • 横然四峙 - Standing majestically on all sides.
  • 山门花辟 - The entrance gate is adorned with flowers.
  • 曲径烟矗 - A winding path surrounded by mist.
  • 延弇升巨亭之上 - Inviting 韦弇 to ascend the large pavilion.
  • 廻廊环构 - A circular corridor encircling the pavilion.
  • 饰以珠玉 - Adorned with pearls and jade.
  • 仙子十数 - Dozens of fairy maidens.
  • 左右侍衞 - Attendants on either side.
  • 华裾靓妆 - Elegant and beautiful attire.
  • 愿聊奉一醉 - Offering a brief drinking session.
  • 无以延欵为疑也 - Not doubting the hospitality.
  • 张乐饮酒 - Playing music and drinking.
  • 陈设肴膳 - Arranging food and dishes.

[410]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嘗金石絲竹雅音清唱 - Compound expression listing various musical instruments and refined musical performances. '金石' refers to percussion and stringed instruments, '絲竹' refers to string and bamboo wind instruments, '雅音' means refined or elegant music, and '清唱' refers to solo singing or a cappella performance.
  • 非世之所聞 - Not heard of in the world or unknown to the world, meaning something rare or unheard of.
  • 乘間問曰 - Seized the opportunity to ask or took the chance to inquire, indicating a moment of privacy or opportunity for questioning.
  • 尊嚴侯家繁盛莫不见之 - The dignity of the noble houses and their prosperity have all been seen. This is a descriptive clause that highlights the splendor and grandeur of the imperial and aristocratic world.
  • 今之所覩固不可偕矣 - What is seen now cannot be compared to what I have seen. Indicates a contrast between past grandeur and present circumstances.
  • 余非人间人 - I am not of the mortal world or I am not of this world, indicating the speaker's supernatural or celestial origin.
  • 玉清仙府 - Jade Clear Celestial Palace, a term for a heavenly or spiritual abode.
  • 适欲奉召假以郑氏之亭耳 - I have just been summoned to serve, and I am borrowing the Zheng family's pavilion for this purpose. Indicates a temporary or borrowed location for a divine or celestial task.
  • 余有新曲名曰紫云 - I have a new song called *Zi Yun* (Purple Cloud), referring to a newly composed piece of music or melody.
  • 今天子奉尚神仙之道 - The present emperor upholds the Way of the Immortals, indicating the emperor's patronage of Daoist or spiritual practices.
  • 余以此乐授于吾子而贡于圣唐之君以此相托可乎 - I would like to entrust this music to your son and present it to the sage ruler of the Tang dynasty. Would you agree to this? A formal request to pass on the music to the emperor through an intermediary.
  • 某一儒生耳 - I am merely a scholar, expressing the speaker's humility.
  • 在长安中区区于九陌以干一名望天子门不可见 - I am in Chang’an, striving in the nine thoroughfares to attain a name, but I cannot see the gate of the Son of Heaven. Indicates the speaker’s low status and inability to access the imperial court.
  • 又非知音者 - Nor am I a connoisseur of music, emphasizing the speaker's lack of expertise in the arts.
  • 若将贡新曲固不可为也 - If I were to offer this new song, it would certainly be inappropriate. Indicates the speaker's self-deprecation regarding his ability to present the song.
  • 君既不能余当寓梦而授于天子 - Since you cannot do it, I shall bestow it upon the emperor through a dream. Indicates a magical or supernatural method of transmission.
  • 愿以三寳为赠子其售之可毕世之富也 - I will give you the Three Treasures; if you sell them, you will gain wealth for a lifetime. Refers to the concept of the 'Three Treasures' in Daoist or Buddhist tradition, which may be symbolic or literal.
  • 饮毕命侍者出一杯谓之碧瑶杯 - After drinking, she ordered the attendant to bring out a cup called the *Bi Yao Cup*. Refers to a specific, possibly magical or precious drinking vessel.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 金石絲竹 - Refers to traditional Chinese musical instruments: '金' (metal, e.g., bells or gongs), '石' (stone, e.g., chimes), '絲' (stringed instruments, e.g., zithers), '竹' (bamboo wind instruments, e.g., flutes).
  • 雅音清唱 - Refers to refined or classical music, often associated with Confucian ideals of harmony and propriety.
  • 上国 - Northern country or the northern capital, often used to refer to the northern regions of China, especially in the context of the Tang dynasty.
  • 二京 - Refers to the two capitals of the Han dynasty: Chang’an and Luoyang. In later contexts, it may refer to the two capitals of the Tang dynasty: Chang’an and Luoyang.
  • 帝宅 - Imperial residence or the palace of the emperor, indicating the seat of imperial power.
  • 尊嚴侯家 - The dignity of the noble houses, referring to the aristocracy and their grandeur.
  • 玉清仙府 - Jade Clear Celestial Palace, a term for a heavenly or Daoist abode, often associated with immortals or celestial beings.
  • 郑氏之亭 - Zheng family's pavilion, a specific location or structure, possibly a pavilion owned by the Zheng family, which may be a historical or literary reference.
  • 紫云 - Purple Cloud, the name of a new song or musical piece. In Chinese tradition, 'purple' is often associated with royalty or the heavens.
  • 今天子 - The present emperor, referring to the ruler of the Tang dynasty.
  • 神仙之道 - The Way of the Immortals, a reference to Daoist spiritual practices or beliefs.
  • 圣唐之君 - The sage ruler of the Tang dynasty, a reverential term for the emperor.
  • 儒生 - A scholar or Confucian scholar, a common designation for educated men in the Tang dynasty.
  • 长安 - Chang’an, the capital of the Tang dynasty and one of the most important cities in Chinese history.
  • 九陌 - Nine thoroughfares or nine streets, a poetic way of referring to the city’s main roads or avenues.
  • 天子门不可见 - The gate of the Son of Heaven cannot be seen, a metaphor for the inaccessibility of the imperial court.
  • 知音者 - A connoisseur of music, a term often used to refer to someone who truly understands or appreciates music, especially in the context of the famous story of the 'knowledge of music' between Cao Cao and the musician Cai Wenji.
  • 三寳 - Three Treasures, a term with multiple meanings in Chinese culture: in Buddhism, it refers to the Buddha, Dharma, and Sangha; in Daoism, it can refer to the Dao, the breath of life, and the spirit. In this context, it may be a magical or symbolic item.
  • 碧瑶杯 - Bi Yao Cup, a name for a specific cup, possibly magical or precious, often found in Daoist or fairy-tale contexts.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 光莹洞彻 - Describes something as brilliantly clear and transparent.
  • 红防枕 - A red jade pillow, with '红防' possibly referring to a specific type or color of jade.
  • 粟其文 - The texture or pattern on the pillow resembles grains of rice.
  • 微红而光彩莹朗 - Slightly red with a brilliant and clear luster.
  • 紫玉函 - A purple jade box, similar in appearance to cloth but with a jade-like quality.
  • 光彩甚于玉 - The luster of the box is even more brilliant than that of jade.
  • 弇拜而谢之 - Yanyan bowed and thanked the person who gave him the items.
  • 行未及一里 - He walked less than one li (about 500 meters).
  • 榛而已 - Only thorny bushes remained.
  • 挈寳入长安 - Carrying the treasures into Chang'an (the capital).
  • 下第 - Failed the imperial examination.
  • 东游广陵 - Traveling east to Guangling (modern-day Yangzhou).
  • 胡商诣弇 - A foreign merchant visited Yanyan.
  • 访其寳 - To inquire about the treasures.
  • 以数十万金易而求之 - Offered tens of thousands of gold coins to buy them.
  • 大富 - Became very wealthy.
  • 筑室江都 - Built a house in Jiangdu (another name for Yangzhou).
  • 竟不求闻达 - Eventually stopped seeking fame and recognition.
  • 不知所终焉 - His final fate is unknown.
  • 持乐器集于庭 - Holding musical instruments, they gathered in the courtyard.
  • 奏曲以授请 - Playing a tune to offer to the emperor.
  • 请为中原正始之音 - Requested it to be the pure and correct music for the Central Plains.
  • 曲名紫云 - The name of the tune was 'Purple Cloud.'
  • 晨兴即以玉笛吹而习之 - In the morning, the emperor immediately played the tune on a jade flute and practiced it.
  • 传于乐府 - The tune was passed down to the Music Bureau.
  • 符弇之所遇 - Coincided with Yanyan's experience.
  • 欲使弇上奏之曲也 - Wanted Yanyan to perform the tune for the emperor.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 乐器 - Musical instruments, including the jade flute.

  • 正始之音 - The pure and correct music, a term often used in classical Chinese to denote the ideal or standard form of music.

  • 留连论道 - Means to linger in discussion, particularly about philosophical or spiritual matters.
  • 动移晷刻 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'moving and shifting for long periods,' indicating that the discussions are lengthy and engaging.
  • 朝闻道夕死可矣 - A famous Confucian saying meaning 'if one can hear the Way in the morning, it is acceptable to die at night,' emphasizing the value of immediate enlightenment.

  • 百年后还得复生 - Means 'after a hundred years, one can be reborn,' indicating the cyclical nature of life and death in Daoist thought.
  • 魏时人 - Refers to someone from the Wei dynasty, a period in Chinese history known for its cultural and philosophical developments.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 世為縣小吏 - Has been a minor official in the county for generations. '世' (shi) indicates a long-standing tradition or lineage, and '縣小吏' (xian xiaoli) refers to a low-ranking government official.
  • 攻文學治道術 - Studies literature and political philosophy. '攻' (gong) means to study or specialize in, '文學' (wenxue) refers to literature, and '治道術' (zhidao shu) refers to political philosophy or governance.
  • 遍游天下 - Travels extensively throughout the world. '遍' (bian) means 'throughout' or 'completely,' and '游' (you) means 'to travel.'
  • 墜霅溪 - Falls into the Zhe River. '墜' (zhui) means 'to fall,' and '霅溪' (zhe xi) is a specific river in Zhejiang Province.
  • 經日方出 - Stays in the water for a whole day before coming out. '經日' (jing ri) means 'for a whole day,' and '方出' (fang chu) means 'finally comes out.'
  • 衣不沾濕 - Clothes do not get wet. '衣' (yi) means 'clothes,' and '不沾濕' (bu zhan shi) means 'do not get wet.'
  • 坐於水上 - Sits on the water. '坐' (zuo) means 'to sit,' and '水上' (shui shang) means 'on the water.'
  • 適為項羽相召飲酒 - Just happened to be summoned by Xiang Yu for a drink. '適' (shi) means 'just' or 'happened to,' '為' (wei) means 'by,' '項羽' (xiang yu) is a historical figure, '相召' (xiang zhao) means 'to summon,' and '飲酒' (yin jiu) means 'to drink.'
  • 欲大醉方返溪滨 - Wants to get very drunk before returning to the riverbank. '欲' (yu) means 'to want,' '大醉' (da zui) means 'very drunk,' '方返' (fang fan) means 'finally returns,' and '溪滨' (xi bin) means 'riverbank.'
  • 觀者如堵 - Spectators like a wall. '觀者' (guan zhe) means 'spectators,' and '如堵' (ru du) means 'like a wall.'
  • 酒气犹冲人 - The smell of alcohol still permeates the air. '酒气' (jiu qi) means 'smell of alcohol,' and '犹冲人' (you chong ren) means 'still permeates the air.'
  • 状若风狂 - Looks like he is crazy. '状' (zhuang) means 'appearance,' and '若风狂' (ruo feng kuang) means 'like being crazy.'
  • 路人多随看之 - Many passersby follow to watch him. '路人' (lu ren) means 'passersby,' '多' (duo) means 'many,' '随看' (sui kan) means 'follow to watch,' and '之' (zhi) is a pronoun referring to Ma Ziran.
  • 时复以拳入鼻 - Sometimes sticks his fist into his nose. '时复' (shi fu) means 'sometimes,' '以拳' (yi quan) means 'with his fist,' and '入鼻' (ru bi) means 'into his nose.'
  • 及出拳鼻如故 - When he takes his fist out, his nose is as it was. '及' (ji) means 'when,' '出拳' (chu quan) means 'takes his fist out,' and '鼻如故' (bi ru gu) means 'his nose is as it was.'
  • 指溪水令逆流 - Points to the river and makes it flow backward. '指' (zhi) means 'to point,' '溪水' (xi shui) means 'river,' '令' (ling) means 'to make,' and '逆流' (ni liu) means 'flow backward.'
  • 食顷指栁树令随溪水来去 - After a meal, he points to a willow tree and makes it move with the river. '食顷' (shi qing) means 'after a meal,' '指' (zhi) means 'to point,' '栁树' (liu shu) means 'willow tree,' '令' (ling) means 'to make,' '随' (sui) means 'to follow,' '溪水' (xi shui) means 'river,' and '来去' (lai qu) means 'move.'
  • 指桥令断复续 - Points to a bridge and makes it break and then restore. '指' (zhi) means 'to point,' '桥' (qiao) means 'bridge,' '令断' (duan) means 'break,' and '复续' (fu xu) means 'restore.'
  • 后游常州防唐宰相马植谪官量移常州刺史 - Later travels to Changzhou and meets the Tang chancellor Ma Zhi, who has been demoted and transferred to Changzhou as a prefect. '后' (hou) means 'later,' '游' (you) means 'to travel,' '常州' (chang zhou) is a place name, '防' (fang) means 'to meet,' '唐宰相' (tang zai xiang) means 'Tang chancellor,' '马植' (ma zhi) is a person's name, '谪官' (zhe guan) means 'demoted official,' '量移' (liang yi) means 'transferred,' and '刺史' (ci shi) means 'prefect.'
  • 素闻湘名乃邀相见 - Having heard of Ma Ziran's name, invites him to meet. '素' (su) means 'previously,' '闻' (wen) means 'to hear,' '湘名' (xiang ming) means 'Ma Ziran's name,' '乃' (nai) means 'then,' '邀' (yao) means 'to invite,' and '相见' (xiang jian) means 'to meet.'
  • 延礼甚异之 - Extends great hospitality and treats him very differently. '延' (yan) means 'to extend,' '礼' (li) means 'hospitality,' '甚' (shen) means 'very,' '异' (yi) means 'different,' and '之' (zhi) is a pronoun referring to Ma Ziran.
  • 植问曰 - Ma Zhi asks. '植' (zhi) is a pronoun referring to Ma Zhi, '问' (wen) means 'to ask,' and '曰' (yue) means 'says.'
  • 幸与道兄同姓 - Fortunately, we share the same surname. '幸' (xing) means 'fortunately,' '与' (yu) means 'with,' '道兄' (dao xiong) means 'fellow practitioner,' and '同姓' (tong xing) means 'share the same surname.'
  • 欲为兄弟冀师道术可乎 - Want to be brothers and hope to learn your Daoist techniques, is that possible? '欲' (yu) means 'to want,' '为' (wei) means 'to be,' '兄弟' (xiong di) means 'brothers,' '冀' (ji) means 'hope,' '师' (shi) means 'to learn from,' '道术' (dao shu) means 'Daoist techniques,' and '可乎' (ke hu) means 'is that possible?'

Proper Nouns

  • 馬自然 - Name '馬自然' (Ma Ziran). '馬' (Ma) is the surname, and '自然' (Ziran) can be translated as 'Natural.' In Classical Chinese, names often carry deeper meanings or cultural significance. '自然' can also imply a state of being in harmony with nature or spontaneity.
  • 項羽 - Historical figure Xiang Yu.
  • 馬植 - Person's name Ma Zhi.
  • 唐宰相 - Tang chancellor.
  • 常州 - Place name Changzhou.
  • 刺史 - Prefect.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 食畧无揖者 - Literally 'eating without bowing,' but likely means 'eating without ceremony' or 'eating without formalities.'
  • 资以饭 - Literally 'providing with food,' but likely means 'offering food.'
  • 促知微延叟急食而去 - A complex phrase indicating that the two monks, Zhiwei and Yansou, were urged to eat quickly and leave.
  • 僧斋未毕及出门又促速行 - Means 'the monks had not finished their meal when they were again urged to leave quickly.'
  • 到诸暨县南店中 - Means 'arrived at a south-side inn in Zhujixian county.'
  • 约去禅院七十余里 - Means 'about seventy miles from the Zen temple.'
  • 深夜闻寻道士声 - Means 'heard the sound of searching for a Taoist in the middle of the night.'
  • 主人遽应 - Means 'the innkeeper quickly responded.'
  • 此有三人 - Means 'there are three people here.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This sentence is elliptical and must be read as a command to the young master to take the solar mirror bead, which will be exchanged for a large sum of money by foreigners.
  • This sequence describes the maid opening the jade box, taking the bead, and giving it to Wei, who then bows twice and receives it with both hands.
  • This rhetorical question asks why Wei, who has never visited the emperor and is not a relative, would suddenly receive such a gift.
  • The woman explains that Wei's ancestor wrote a poem on Yue Terrace that inspired the repair of the terrace.
  • The emperor felt ashamed and wrote a poem in response, revealing his intention to bestow the bead, without needing the woman to explain it to Wei.
  • The woman commands the maid to write the title on the pen of the messenger from Guangzhou.
  • This poetic expression describes the Yue Terrace crumbling at the roadside, the governor reapplying the red coating, and rewarding the effort with a beautiful woman and a pearl.
  • The woman tells Wei to prepare fine wine and abundant food in the quiet room of Pu Jian Temple in Guangzhou, where they will send Lady Tian.
  • Wei then bows twice and leaves, wanting to follow the messenger's sheep.
  • The woman tells Wei about Bo Gu's mugwort and suggests he leave a little bit.
  • Wei just leaves the mugwort and does not know who Bo Gu is, so he leaves it and soon.

  • 胡人 - Foreigners.

  • 玉函 - Jade box.

  • 行在 - Refers to the location of the imperial court.
  • 兴元 - Refers to the Xingyuan era, a period during the Tang Dynasty.

[471]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 陜虢防御使 - A title referring to the military governor of the regions of Shaan and Guo (modern-day Henan and Shaanxi), responsible for defense and administration.
  • 陈许戍卒三千自京西逃归至陜州界 - A complex clause meaning 'the 3,000 soldiers from Chen and Xu who had fled from the western capital (Chang'an) returned to the Shaanzhou border.'
  • 泌潜师险隘尽破之 - 'Bi secretly deployed troops to the dangerous passes and completely defeated them.' '潜师' is a military term meaning 'to secretly deploy troops.'
  • 开三门陆运一十八里漕米无砥柱之患 - 'opened the land transport route of Sanmen (three gates) for 18 li, allowing grain transport without the danger of the Dizhu (a rock formation in the Yellow River).' '砥柱' refers to a famous rock in the Yellow River, known as the 'Pillar of the River,' which posed a danger to漕运 (grain transport).
  • 大济京师 - 'greatly aided the capital city (Chang'an).'
  • 就拜中书侍郎平章事 - A title and honorific granted by the emperor, meaning 'was immediately appointed as the Secretary of the Central Secretariat and Chancellor of State Affairs.'
  • 加崇文馆大学士修国史 - 'was also granted the title of Grand Academician of the Chongwen Hall and given the duty of compiling the national history.'
  • 封邺侯 - 'was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Ye.'
  • 时顺宗在春宫妃萧氏母郜国公主交通于外 - 'At that time, Emperor Shunzong was in the spring palace (i.e., the eastern palace where the heir apparent resided), and his consort, Lady Xiao, and her mother, Princess Gao of the Guo state, were involved in external intrigues.'
  • 上疑其有他志 - 'the emperor suspected them of having other intentions.'
  • 连坐贬黜者数人 - 'several people were demoted or dismissed due to the principle of collective punishment (lian zu).'
  • 皇储危惧 - 'the heir apparent was in fear and danger.'
  • 泌周旋陈奏 - 'Bi mediated and submitted petitions.'
  • 颇有谠正之风 - 'he had a strong sense of uprightness and integrity.'
  • 泌奏今有司上农书献穜稑之种 - 'Bi proposed that the officials should now submit agricultural books and offer new grain seeds.'
  • 王公戚里上春服 - 'nobles and imperial relatives should present spring clothing.'
  • 士庶乃各相问讯 - 'the common people and officials would greet each other.'
  • 泌又作中和酒祭勾芒神以祈年谷 - 'Bi also created Zhonghe wine to offer to the god Goumang in order to pray for a good harvest.'
  • 至今行之 - 'This practice is still carried out today.'
  • 泌旷达敏辨好大言 - 'Bi was open-minded, quick-witted, and fond of grand speeches.'
  • 自出入中禁累为权臣所挤 - 'Since entering the inner court, he had repeatedly been pushed aside by powerful ministers.'
  • 恒由智免 - 'He always escaped through wisdom.'
  • 终以言论纵横上悟圣主以跻相位 - 'In the end, his eloquent speech and arguments convinced the emperor, leading him to the position of chancellor.'
  • 是岁三月薨赠太子太傅 - 'In the third month of that year, he died and was posthumously granted the title of Grand Tutor of the Crown Prince.'
  • 是月中使林远于蓝关逆旅遇泌单骑常服言暂 - 'In that month, the imperial messenger Lin Yuan met Bi at an inn near Lan Pass while Bi was riding alone in plain clothes, saying that he was only passing through briefly.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 陜虢 - Refers to the regions of Shaan and Guo, which were administrative regions during the Tang Dynasty, located in modern-day Henan and Shaanxi.
  • 陈许 - Likely refers to the military garrisons of Chen and Xu, which were strategic regions during the Tang period.
  • 三门 - Refers to Sanmen (Three Gates), a critical passage of the Yellow River near Luoyang, used for grain transport.
  • 砥柱 - A famous rock formation in the Yellow River, known as the 'Pillar of the River,' which was a hazard to漕运 (grain transport).
  • 中书侍郎平章事 - A high-ranking official title in the Tang court, equivalent to a chancellor or prime minister.
  • 崇文馆大学士 - A high academic title in the Tang court, associated with the Chongwen Hall, an institution for literary and historical studies.
  • 修国史 - A duty assigned to scholars to compile and maintain the official history of the dynasty.
  • 封邺侯 - A noble title granted by the emperor, indicating high status and honor.
  • 顺宗 - Emperor Shunzong of Tang, who reigned from 805–806 CE.
  • 春宫 - The eastern palace where the heir apparent resided.
  • 萧氏 - Lady Xiao, the consort of Emperor Shunzong.
  • 郜国公主 - Princess Gao of the Guo state, the mother of Lady Xiao.
  • 交通于外 - Refers to engaging in external intrigues or conspiracies, possibly with officials or factions outside the court.
  • 连坐贬黜 - A legal principle in ancient China where individuals associated with a wrongdoing could be punished as well.
  • 皇储 - The heir apparent, i.e., Emperor Shunzong.
  • 中和节 - A festival established by Emperor Dezong, likely intended to promote harmony and agricultural prosperity.
  • 农书 - Agricultural texts or treatises.
  • 穜稑之种 - Refers to new or improved grain seeds, '穜' being early sown and '稑' being later sown.
  • 春服 - Spring clothing, a traditional custom of presenting new clothing in spring.
  • 士庶 - Scholars and common people.
  • 勾芒神 - The god of spring and agriculture in Chinese mythology, associated with the growth of crops.
  • 年谷 - Refers to the harvest or grain yield.
  • 中禁 - The inner palace or court, where the emperor and his closest advisors resided.
  • 权臣 - Powerful ministers or officials who wielded significant influence.
  • 言论纵横 - Refers to eloquent and persuasive speech.
  • 上悟圣主 - 'the emperor was convinced by his eloquence.'
  • 太子太傅 - A high-ranking title granted posthumously to honor a deceased official, equivalent to a Grand Tutor of the Crown Prince.
  • 蓝关 - Lan Pass, a famous mountain pass in the Qinling Mountains, often referenced in Tang poetry and historical accounts.

[472]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 往衡山話四朝之重遇惨然久之而别 - Visit to Mount Heng to discuss a significant meeting with four emperors, feeling deeply moved and parting after a long time.
  • 遠到长安方闻其薨 - Only learned of someone's death upon arriving in Chang'an.
  • 先生自言当匡佐四圣而复脱屣也 - Statement by a respected scholar that he would assist four emperors and then retire.
  • 服气修道周游名山诣南岳张先生受箓 - Practice of breathing exercises and spiritual cultivation, traveling to famous mountains, and seeking a talisman from Mr. Zhang at Mount Heng.
  • 慎勿多言领取十年宰相 - Warning not to speak too much and prediction of becoming a chancellor in ten years.
  • 每导引骨节皆珊然有声时人谓之鏁子骨 - Sound made by the joints when performing certain exercises, which people called 'lock bones'.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 衡山 - Mount Heng, one of the Five Great Mountains of China, known for its spiritual significance.
  • 四朝 - Refers to the reigns of four emperors, likely from the Tang Dynasty.
  • 南岳张先生 - Mr. Zhang at Mount Heng, a figure associated with Taoist practices and talismans.
  • 郑叔则 - A historical figure, likely a relative or acquaintance of the author.
  • 嬾残 - A monk known as 'Lazy Residue', a character in Chinese literature and folklore.
  • 明心论 - A treatise on the clarity of the mind, likely written by the author or another scholar.

Proper Nouns

  • 衡山 - Mount Heng.
  • 南岳张先生 - Mr. Zhang at Mount Heng.
  • 郑叔则 - A historical figure.
  • 嬾残 - A monk known as 'Lazy Residue'.
  • 明心论 - A treatise on the clarity of the mind.

[473]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 目防然不知人事 - Phrase describing a state of being unaware or unconscious of human affairs.
  • 既寤见身自顶踊出三二寸 - Vivid description of waking up and seeing one's body rising from the top by a few inches.
  • 傍有灵仙挥手动目如相勉助者 - Describes a scene where spirits or immortals are seen moving their hands and eyes as if encouraging or assisting.
  • 如自足及顶乃念言大事未毕复有庭闱之恋 - Phrase indicating that upon realizing the body has risen from the feet to the top, the person thinks about unfinished matters and the affection for family.
  • 愿终家事于是在傍者皆见一人仪状甚巨衣冠如帝王者 - Expresses the wish to complete family matters, and those nearby see a figure of great stature dressed like an emperor.
  • 前有妇人礼服而跪如帝王者责曰情之未得因欲令来使劳灵仙之重跪者对曰不然且教伊近天子 - Describes a woman in formal attire kneeling before the emperor-like figure, who reprimands and expresses a desire to bring someone closer to the emperor, while the kneeling woman responds.
  • 复有庭闱之恋愿终家事 - Reiterates the affection for family and the wish to complete family matters.
  • 后常有者八人容服甚异来过郑家数自言仙法严备事无不至临去叹曰俗縁竟未尽可惜心与骨耳 - Describes eight beings with unusual attire visiting Vang's house, speaking of strict preparation for immortality, and expressing regret that worldly ties are not yet severed.
  • 泌求随去曰不可姑与他为却宰相耳出门不复见因作八公诗叙之 - Describes a request to follow, a refusal, and a promise to become a chancellor instead, followed by a poem about the eight immortals.
  • 复有者携一男六七嵗来过云有故须南行旬月当还縁此男有痢疾既同是道者愿且寄之又留一函曰若疾不起望以此瘗之既许乃问男曰不骄 - Describes another visit by a being with a young boy, explaining a journey, and leaving a letter for burial if the boy dies, followed by a question to the boy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 灵仙 - Refers to immortals or spirits in Chinese mythology.
  • 帝王者 - Refers to an emperor or a figure of imperial status.
  • 庭闱之恋 - Refers to affection for family, particularly in a domestic context.
  • 者 - Refers to beings or immortals with unusual appearances.
  • 八公诗 - Refers to a poem about the eight immortals, a common theme in Chinese literature.
  • 郑家 - Refers to the house of Vang, a specific location in the narrative.
  • 宰相 - Refers to a high-ranking official, equivalent to a chancellor or prime minister.

Proper Nouns

  • 灵仙 - Immortals or spirits.
  • 帝王者 - Emperor or a figure of imperial status.
  • 庭闱之恋 - Affection for family.
  • 者 - Beings or immortals with unusual appearances.
  • 八公诗 - A poem about the eight immortals.
  • 郑家 - The house of Vang.
  • 宰相 - Chancellor or prime minister.

[474]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 留此得乎 - Rhetorical question meaning 'Can one stay here and survive?' or 'Is it possible to remain here and live?'
  • 遂去泌求药疗之 - Means 'then he left and went to seek medicine for his treatment.'
  • 终不愈 - Means 'he eventually did not recover.'
  • 八九日而殂 - Means 'after eight or nine days, he died.'
  • 即以函盛瘗庭中蔷薇架下 - Means 'immediately, he was placed in a coffin and buried in the courtyard under the rose trellis.'
  • 累月其人竟不廻 - Means 'for several months, the person never returned.'
  • 试发函视之 - Means 'when they tried to open the coffin and look inside.'
  • 有一黑石天然中方上有字如锥画 - Means 'there was a black stone with naturally formed square patterns and characters carved like with a cone.'
  • 神真链形年未足化为我子功相续 - Can be interpreted as 'the divine true form, not yet complete in years, transformed into my son, continuing the work.'
  • 何长死何促 - Means 'how long can one live, and how quickly can one die?'
  • 至今效而为之 - Means 'to this day, people follow and do it.'
  • 乃作养和篇以献肃宗 - Means 'he then wrote the 'Nourishing Harmony' chapter to present to Emperor Suzong.'
  • 肃宗 - Refers to Emperor Suzong of Tang.
  • 泌去三四载 - Means 'after three or four years, he left.'
  • 二圣登遐 - Means 'the two emperors passed away.'
  • 代宗践祚 - Means 'Emperor Daizong ascended the throne.'
  • 诏追至阙 - Means 'an edict was issued to summon him to the palace.'
  • 舍于蓬莱殿延喜阁 - Means 'he was housed in the Penglaidean and Yanxige halls.'
  • 给事以上及方镇除降 - Means 'from officials above the rank of Shilang to regional governors and military commanders.'
  • 代宗必令商量军国大事 - Means 'Emperor Daizong always had him consult on major state affairs.'
  • 因语及建宁王灵武之功 - Means 'he then spoke about the achievements of Prince Jianning in Lingwu.'
  • 请加赠太子 - Means 'he requested that Prince Jianning be posthumously honored as a crown prince.'
  • 代宗感悼久之 - Means 'Emperor Daizong was deeply moved and mourned for a long time.'
  • 吾弟之功非先生则世人不知 - Means 'my brother's achievements would not be known to the world without you.'
  • 岂止赠太子也 - Means 'it's not just about posthumous honor as a crown prince.'
  • 引至城门奏以龙輀不动 - Means 'when they brought him to the city gate, the dragon-drawn carriage did not move.'
  • 代宗自蓬莱院谓曰 - Means 'Emperor Daizong, from the Penglaianyuan, said to him.'
  • 吾弟似欲 - Means 'my brother seems to want.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 肃宗 - Refers to Emperor Suzong of Tang (Li Heng), who reigned from 756 to 762.
  • 代宗 - Refers to Emperor Daizong of Tang (Li Yu), who reigned from 762 to 779.
  • 建宁王 - Refers to Li Yi, a prince of the Tang dynasty who played a significant role in the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 灵武 - Refers to Lingwu, a city in Shaanxi Province where Li Yi played a crucial role in the rebellion against An Lushan.
  • 蓬莱殿延喜阁 - Refers to specific halls within the imperial palace.
  • 给事 - Refers to officials of the rank of Shilang, a high-ranking position in the Tang government.
  • 方镇 - Refers to regional military governors.
  • 龙輀 - Refers to a ceremonial carriage used for transporting the deceased emperor.

Proper Nouns

  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang.
  • 代宗 - Emperor Daizong of Tang.
  • 建宁王 - Li Yi, a prince of the Tang dynasty.
  • 灵武 - A city in Shaanxi Province.
  • 蓬莱殿延喜阁 - Specific halls within the imperial palace.
  • 给事 - Officials of the rank of Shilang.
  • 方镇 - Regional military governors.
  • 龙輀 - A ceremonial carriage used for transporting the deceased emperor.

[475]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 見先生宜速往酹祝兼宣朕意 - One should quickly go to pay respects and offer sacrifices, while also conveying my intentions. The verb 酹祝 (pay respects and offer sacrifices) is a compound action that combines reverence and ritual.
  • 追加大號 - Posthumously granting a grand title or honorific. It is a common practice in Chinese imperial history to bestow titles on deceased emperors or officials.
  • 可作一文以传不朽 - One can write an article to ensure immortality. The idea of writing as a way to achieve eternal fame or legacy is a recurring theme in Chinese literature.
  • 挽歌词 - A type of funeral song or poem, often written to mourn the death of a person of high status, such as an emperor.
  • 已发引矣 - The funeral procession has already departed. It indicates that the burial has taken place, and the time for writing a full-length article is missed.
  • 他文不及作 - There is no time to write other articles. It emphasizes the urgency of the situation.
  • 代宗览之而泣 - Emperor Suzong read it and wept. The act of weeping suggests deep emotion, likely due to the content of the poem.
  • 命中人驰授挽者 - He ordered someone to deliver the poem to the funeral procession. The use of 命中人 (ordering someone) indicates the urgency and importance of the task.
  • 定策大功 - The significant achievement of establishing a plan or strategy, often in the context of political or military success.
  • 有图嗣害兄之心 - Had intentions to harm his elder brother and succeed him. It suggests a plot against the emperor, which led to the death of Prince Jianning.
  • 追悟倓无罪 - Suzong later realized that Jianning was innocent. It indicates a change in Suzong's perception after the fact.
  • 虑复及诸王 - Fearing that the same fate might befall other princes. It shows the concern for the safety of other members of the royal family.
  • 因事言 - Taking the opportunity to speak. It suggests that the speaker is using a current event or situation to make a point.
  • 皇祖睿宗最幼 - Emperor Ruizong was the youngest. It identifies the specific son of Emperor Gaozong.
  • 武后生者自为行第 - Those born to Wu Zetian were numbered separately. It explains the unique numbering system for Wu Zetian's children.
  • 皇祖第四长曰孝敬皇帝 - The fourth eldest son of the late emperor was Emperor Xiao Jing. It introduces the character of Emperor Xiao Jing.
  • 监国而仁明 - Who was regent and wise. It describes Emperor Xiao Jing's role and character.
  • 为武后所忌而鸩之 - But was poisoned by Wu Zetian due to her jealousy. It explains the cause of Emperor Xiao Jing's death.
  • 次曰雍王贤 - The second son was Prince Yong, known as Prince Xian. It introduces another character.
  • 为太子中宗睿宗常所不安 - Who was the crown prince but caused discomfort to Emperor Zhongzong and Ruizong. It describes the tension between Prince Xian and the other emperors.
  • 晨夕忧惧 - Constantly worried and fearful. It conveys the emotional state of Prince Xian.
  • 虽父母之前无由敢言 - Even before his parents, he dared not speak. It highlights the constraints on Prince Xian's actions.
  • 欲 - To want or to intend. It indicates the purpose behind Prince Xian's actions.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 代宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang, who reigned from 756 to 762 CE. He is mentioned in the text as the ruler who orders the composition of the funeral poem.
  • 建宁王倓 - Prince Jianning, a son of Emperor Xuanzong, who was falsely accused of plotting against his elder brother and was executed. He is the subject of the posthumous praise in the text.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong's father, Emperor Xuanzong, who reigned from 712 to 756 CE. He is mentioned as the one who falsely accused Prince Jianning.
  • 睿宗 - Emperor Ruizong, who reigned from 710 to 712 CE. He is mentioned as the youngest son of Emperor Gaozong and the brother of Wu Zetian's children.
  • 武后 - Wu Zetian, the only female emperor in Chinese history, who ruled as empress regnant from 690 to 705 CE. She is mentioned as the mother of several emperors and the one who numbered her children separately.
  • 孝敬皇帝 - Emperor Xiao Jing, the fourth eldest son of Emperor Gaozong, who was poisoned by Wu Zetian.
  • 雍王贤 - Prince Yong, also known as Prince Xian, the second son of Emperor Gaozong, who was the crown prince but caused discomfort to the other emperors.
  • 中宗 - Emperor Zhongzong, who reigned from 684 to 690 CE and again from 705 to 710 CE. He is mentioned as one of the emperors who was uncomfortable with Prince Xian.

Proper Nouns

  • 代宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang.
  • 建宁王倓 - Prince Jianning.
  • 肃宗 - Emperor Xuanzong of Tang.
  • 睿宗 - Emperor Ruizong.

[476]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 微悟父母之意 - Gaining a subtle understanding of one's parents' intent.
  • 冀天皇天后闻之歌曰 - Hoping that the Emperor and Empress would hear and sing the song.
  • 慎无再摘 - A direct warning: 'Be cautious not to pick again.' A clear allusion to the metaphor above.
  • 泌请肃宗奉表请归东宫次作功臣表述马嵬灵武之事请上皇还京 - A complex sentence where the minister (Li Bi) requests that Suzong present a formal petition to return the Emperor (Xuanzong) to the capital, citing events from the Battle of Ma嵬 and the establishment of the capital at Lingwu.
  • 肃宗召泌且泣且喜曰上皇已下诰还京皆卿力也 - 'Suzong summoned Li Bi, weeping and joyful, saying that the Emperor had already issued an edict to return to the capital, all of which was due to your efforts.'
  • 一旦凌晨生至窦门颇甚嗟叹庭芝请问良久乃言君家大祸将 - A dramatic passage where the fortune teller appears at dawn and delivers a dire prophecy to Dou Tingzhi about impending disaster for his family.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 肃宗 - Emperor Suzong of Tang, who ruled during the An Lushan Rebellion and was the son of Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 李泌 - A famous Tang minister and strategist, known for his political acumen and close relationship with both Emperor Suzong and Emperor Xuanzong.
  • 马嵬 - A historical site where Emperor Xuanzong and his wife, Yang Guifei, were forced to flee during the An Lushan Rebellion.
  • 灵武 - A city where Emperor Suzong established his base and proclaimed himself emperor during the rebellion.
  • 员外郎 - An official title in the Tang bureaucracy, meaning 'assistant to the official in charge of external affairs.'
  • 分司洛邑 - 'Assigned to Luoyang' — a reference to the administrative division of the Tang bureaucracy, where officials were stationed in different cities.
  • 卜者 - A fortune teller or diviner.
  • 葫芦生 - A name for the fortune teller, likely a nickname or pseudonym.
  • 窦庭芝 - A Tang official mentioned in the text, who consulted the fortune teller.
  • 大祸将 - 'A great disaster is coming' — a dire prophecy delivered by the fortune teller.

[477]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 成举家啼泣请问求生之路 - A dramatic expression describing the entire family crying and pleading for a way to survive.
  • 若非遇中黄君 - A conditional phrase meaning 'if it were not for meeting Zhong Huangjun.'
  • 但见谷子 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'only seeing the ghostly child,' referring to a child with a rare or unusual condition.
  • 亦可无患矣 - A conclusion indicating that there would be no harm.
  • 生乃具述形貌服餙 - A detailed description of appearance and attire.
  • 仍戒以浃旬求之 - An instruction to search for the child over ten days.
  • 晓夕求访殆遍洛下 - A phrase indicating that the search was conducted day and night, covering almost all of Luoyang.
  • 时泌居于河清 - A temporal clause indicating that at that time, Bi lived in Heqing.
  • 因省亲友 - A phrase meaning 'because of visiting relatives and friends.'
  • 策蹇入洛 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'riding a slow, old horse into Luoyang.'
  • 至中桥 - A place name, 'to the Middle Bridge.'
  • 遇京尹避道 - A phrase indicating encountering the governor who had to yield the way.
  • 所乘骡忽惊轶而走 - A vivid description of the governor's horse suddenly becoming startled and running away.
  • 径入庭芝所居 - A phrase indicating the horse ran directly into the residence of Ting Zhi.
  • 与仆者共造其门 - A phrase indicating going together to the door.
  • 车马罗列将出 - A phrase describing the arrangement of carriages and horses, ready to leave.
  • 忽见泌 - A sudden appearance of Bi.
  • 皆惊愕而退 - A phrase indicating everyone was surprised and retreated.
  • 俄有人云 - A phrase indicating someone soon said.
  • 分司窦员外宅所失骡在马廐 - A phrase indicating that the lost horse and mule from the office of the external officer Duan were found in the stable.
  • 请客入座 - A polite invitation to enter and sit.
  • 主人当愿修谒 - A phrase indicating the host wishes to make a formal visit.
  • 泌不得已就其防庭 - A phrase indicating Bi had no choice but to go to the host's courtyard.
  • 庭芝既出降阶再拜延接慇懃 - A phrase describing Ting Zhi coming out, descending the steps, bowing twice, and warmly welcoming Bi.
  • 遂至信宿 - A phrase indicating staying for two nights.
  • 至于妻子咸备家人之礼数 - A phrase indicating that Bi's wife and children were all treated with family-like respect.
  • 赠遗殊厚 - A phrase indicating generous gifts.
  • 但云遭遇之辰愿以一家奉托 - A phrase indicating that the host said, 'On this fortunate occasion, I wish to entrust my entire family to you.'
  • 时泌居于河清 - A temporal clause indicating that at that time, Bi lived in Heqing.
  • 信使旁午于道 - A phrase indicating that messengers were coming and going constantly on the road.
  • 庭芝初与泌相值 - A phrase indicating that Ting Zhi first met Bi.
  • 葫芦生适在其家 - A phrase indicating that Hu Lu Sheng happened to be at his home.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '子往视之不复见巨子便随鹤而去' is a sequence of actions and observations, which must be read as a single event.
  • The phrase '冉冉而灭' describes the gradual disappearance of something, which must be read as a single action.
  • The conditional statement '此是妖讹事必汝父得仙' must be read as a single logical unit.
  • The conditional statement '吾境内苦旱当为致雨不雨杀汝子' must be read as a single logical unit.
  • The sequence of actions '归焚香上陈怀仙使金叅军赍酒脯至巨宅致祭' must be read as a single event.
  • The phrase '其日大雨远近皆足' indicates a result of the previous actions, which must be read as a single event.
  • The phrase '以所求灵验乃于巨宅立庙歳时享祀焉' indicates a result of the previous events, which must be read as a single event.

  • 亦當委順 - Means 'one should also accept it,' suggesting resignation to fate.

  • 汶又留半月 - Means 'Wen kept him for another half a month,' indicating Wen's continued efforts to retain the person.
  • 此須去矣 - Means 'it is time to leave now,' indicating the inevitability of departure.

  • 其后诸相识人家皆云同日见李山人来吿别 - Means 'after that, all the acquaintances in various households said they saw Mr. Li coming to say goodbye on the same day,' indicating a widespread observation.

  • 乃云十五年后于昆明池边见人家小儿颊有疵者即某身也 - Means 'he then said, 'In fifteen years, at the edge of Kunming Pool, you will see a child with a blemish on his cheek; that will be me,' indicating a prophetic statement.
  • 乃行其后亦不知所之 - Means 'he then left, and no one knew where he went,' indicating the mysterious disappearance of the person.

Idiomatic Expressions

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 夫道者可以心得岂在力争悭惜未止术无由成当须深山穷谷勤求致之非市朝所授也 - The way of Dao can be attained through the heart, not through fierce contention or stinginess; if one does not stop being stingy, the art cannot be achieved. It must be sought diligently in remote mountains and valleys, not taught in the marketplace, with '夫道者' meaning 'the way of Dao.'
  • 贺意颇悟谢之而去 - The guest understood deeply and thanked him before leaving, with '颇悟' meaning to understand deeply.
  • 数日失老人所在 - Several days later, the old man's whereabouts were lost, with '失' meaning to lose track of.
  • 贺因求致仕入道还乡 - The guest then requested retirement and returned home to practice Dao, with '致仕' meaning to retire from office.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '持一明珠自云在乡日得此珠保惜多时特上老人求説道法' must be read as a single action: carrying a pearl, saying he obtained it in his hometown, valuing it for a long time, and then going to an old man to seek teachings.
  • The sentence '老人即以明珠付童子令市饼来童子以珠易得三十余胡饼遂延贺贺私念寳珠特以轻用意甚不快' must be read as a series of actions: the old man gives the pearl to a child, the child exchanges it for bread, and then the guest is invited, with the guest privately thinking the pearl was used too lightly.
  • The sentence '夫道者可以心得岂在力争悭惜未止术无由成当须深山穷谷勤求致之非市朝所授也' must be read as a single argument: the way of Dao can be attained through the heart, not through fierce contention or stinginess; if one does not stop being stingy, the art cannot be achieved. It must be sought diligently in remote mountains and valleys, not taught in the marketplace.
  • The sentence '贺意颇悟谢之而去数日失老人所在贺因求致仕入道还乡' must be read as a series of actions: the guest understands deeply, thanks the old man, and leaves; several days later, the old man's whereabouts are lost, and the guest requests retirement and returns home to practice Dao.
  • The sentence '萧颖士常密游于陈留逆旅方食之次忽见老翁须鬂皓然眉目尤异至门目萧久之微有叹息又似相识' must be read as a single observation: Xiao Yingshi was secretly traveling to Chenliu, eating at an inn, when he suddenly saw an old man with white beard and eyebrows, extraordinary eyes, and a gaze that lingered on him, with a slight sigh, as if they had met before.
  • The sentence '萧疑其意遂起揖问观郎君状貌有似一人不觉怆然耳' must be read as a series of actions: Xiao suspected the old man's intentions, rose to bow and ask, and the old man observed that Xiao looked like someone, causing him to feel a pang of sadness.
  • The sentence '萧问似何人老人曰郎君一似齐鄱阳王王即萧八代祖遂惊问曰王即某八代祖因何识之' must be read as a series of questions and answers: Xiao asked who the old man was, and the old man said he looked like Qi Prince Poyang, who is Xiao's eighth-generation ancestor. Xiao was startled and asked how the old man knew him.
  • The sentence '老人泣曰某姓左昔为鄱阳书佐偏宠遇遭李明之难遂尔逃亡茍免患耳因入山修道遂得度世适惊郎君乃不知是王孙也遂相与泣' must be read as a single explanation: the old man wept and explained his name, his past service, his escape from troubles, and his achievement of immortality, and they both wept together.
  • The sentence '萧敬异之问其年乃三百二十七年矣良久乃别今在灊山时出人间后不知所之' must be read as a series of actions: Xiao was greatly surprised, asked the old man's age, which turned out to be 327 years. After a long time, they parted, and the old man is now in Qianshan, occasionally appearing in the human world, with his final destination unknown.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 者有大祸幸君侯无犯仙官之约 - A classical expression indicating that someone is about to face great misfortune unless they avoid breaking an agreement with a divine or supernatural entity.
  • 崔君怒曰 - Indicates that Cui Jun (崔君) is speaking angrily.
  • 尹真人死千嵗安得独有石函在乎 - A rhetorical question suggesting that since the immortal Yin Zhenren (尹真人) has been dead for a thousand years, it is unlikely that he alone would have a stone coffin.
  • 吾不信顔道士其词而崔君固不从 - Expresses Cui Jun's disbelief in the words of Daoist Master Yan (顔道士) and his refusal to listen.
  • 于是命破其鏁久之而坚然不可动 - Indicates that Cui Jun ordered the lock to be broken, but after a long time, it remained immovable.
  • 崔君怒又以巨絙系函鼻用数十牛拽其絙鞭而驱之 - Describes Cui Jun's anger and his decision to use a large rope tied to the coffin's handle and dozens of oxen to pull it.
  • 仅半日石函遂开 - Indicates that after only half a day, the stone coffin was opened.
  • 中有符箓数十轴以黄缣为幅丹书其文皆炳然如新 - Describes the contents of the coffin: dozens of scrolls of talismans written on yellow silk with red ink, all appearing as fresh as new.
  • 崔君既观毕顾谓顔道士曰 - Indicates that after viewing the contents, Cui Jun turned to Daoist Master Yan and said.
  • 吾向者意函中有奇寳故开而阅之今徒有符箓而已 - Expresses Cui Jun's realization that he had expected to find a rare treasure but only found talismans.
  • 于是令缄鏁如旧 - Indicates that Cui Jun ordered the coffin to be sealed and locked as it was.
  • 既归郡是夕令忽暴卒后三日而寤其官属将吏辈悉诣崔君问之且讯焉 - Describes the events that followed: Cui Jun returned to the prefecture, died suddenly that night, and after three days, he woke up, with his officials and subordinates all coming to ask him about what happened.
  • 崔君曰吾甚戅未尝闻 - Indicates that Cui Jun said he was very foolish and had never heard of such things before.
  • 神仙事前者偶开尹真人石函果为防官追摄 - Describes the events that led to Cui Jun's death: he had opened the stone coffin of the immortal Yin Zhenren and was subsequently pursued by officials from the celestial realm.
  • 初见一人衣紫衣至寝谓吾曰我吏于防司者也今奉命召君固不可拒拒则祸益大矣宜疾去 - Describes the encounter with a person in purple clothing who told Cui Jun that he was an official from the celestial realm and had been ordered to summon him, warning him that refusal would bring greater disaster and urging him to leave quickly.
  • 吾始闻忧欲以辞免然不觉与使者俱出郡城仅行五十里至防司其官即故相吕公也 - Describes Cui Jun's initial reaction and the journey to the celestial realm, where he met the former prime minister Lu Gong (吕公).
  • 谓吾曰子何为开尹真人石函乎奉上帝命且削君之禄寿果如何哉 - Describes the former prime minister's question to Cui Jun about why he had opened the stone coffin of the immortal Yin Zhenren and the consequences of doing so.
  • 已而召掾吏至令按吾禄寿之籍掾吏白吕公曰崔君有官五任有寿十七年今奉上帝符尽夺五任官又削十五年寿今独有二年任矣 - Describes the process of checking Cui Jun's official records and the decision to reduce his lifespan and official positions.
  • 于是听崔君还后二年果卒 - Indicates that Cui Jun was allowed to return after two years, but he eventually died.

  • 李氏妻 - The central character in the story. No further historical identity is given.
  • 东晋邺下 - 'In the Eastern Jin, at Yecheng (邺下),' referring to the city of Yecheng (modern-day Handan, Hebei), a significant cultural and political center during the Jin dynasty.
  • 葛仙君 - Ge Xianjun (葛仙君), a Daoist immortal and figure of legend, often associated with healing, alchemy, and spiritual transformation. He is one of the 'Eight Immortals' in some traditions.
  • 寿尽之日无形尔 - This reflects Daoist and Buddhist notions of the soul’s state after death, where the soul becomes formless until rebirth.
  • 神胶 - A term used in Daoist and alchemical texts for a supernatural substance capable of binding or reconstituting physical forms.
  • 仙传拾遗 - The source text is cited as coming from *Xian Chuan Shi Yi* (仙傳拾遺), which translates to 'Supplement to the Immortal Transmissions' or 'Supplement to the Immortal Biographies.' This is likely a collection of Daoist legends and immortality tales.

  • 邺下 - Yecheng (邺下), the former capital of the Later Zhao and a cultural center during the Jin dynasty.
  • 田先生 - 'Master Tian' (田先生), a Daoist sage or spiritual guide in the story.
  • 仙传拾遗 - *Xian Chuan Shi Yi* (仙傳拾遺), the source text of this legend, a Daoist collection of immortal biographies.
